heading: (intro)
raw: Directed by John Ford. Written by Frank Nugent.
clean: Directed by John Ford. Written by Frank Nugent.

heading: Ethan Edwards
raw: I figure a man's only good for one oath at a time. I gave mine to the Confederate States of America.
clean: I figure a man's only good for one oath at a time. I gave mine to the Confederate States of America.

heading: Ethan Edwards
raw: Well, Reverend, that tears it! From now on, you stay out of this. All of ya. I don't want you with me. I don't need ya for what I got to do.
clean: Well, Reverend, that tears it! From now on, you stay out of this. All of ya. I don't want you with me. I don't need ya for what I got to do.

heading: Ethan Edwards
raw: Let's go home, Debbie.
clean: Let's go home, Debbie.

heading: Ethan Edwards
raw: [repeated line] That'll be the day !
clean: That'll be the day !

heading: Mrs. Jorgensen
raw: It just so happens we be Texicans. Texican is nothin' but a human man way out on a limb, this year and next. Maybe for a hundred more. But I don't think it'll be forever. Some day, this country's gonna be a fine good place to be. Maybe it needs our bones in the ground before that time can come.
clean: It just so happens we be Texicans. Texican is nothin' but a human man way out on a limb, this year and next. Maybe for a hundred more. But I don't think it'll be forever. Some day, this country's gonna be a fine good place to be. Maybe it needs our bones in the ground before that time can come.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Ethan Edwards: Well, Reverend, looks like you've got yourself surrounded.
clean: Well, Reverend, looks like you've got yourself surrounded.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Reverend Clayton: Yeah, and I figure on getting myself unsurrounded.
clean: Yeah, and I figure on getting myself unsurrounded.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Reverend Clayton: I say we do it my way. That's an order!
clean: I say we do it my way. That's an order!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Ethan: Yessir. But if you're wrong don't ever give me another.
clean: Yessir. But if you're wrong don't ever give me another.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Reverend Clayton: You wanna quit Ethan?
clean: You wanna quit Ethan?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Ethan: That'll be the day.
clean: That'll be the day.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Cavalry soldier: It's hard to believe they're white.
clean: It's hard to believe they're white.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Ethan: They ain't white, not anymore. They're Comanche.
clean: They ain't white, not anymore. They're Comanche.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Ethan: What you saw wasn't Lucy... What you saw was a buck wearin' Lucy's dress. I found Lucy back in the canyon. Wrapped her in my coat, buried her with my own hands, I thought it best to keep it from ya.
clean: What you saw wasn't Lucy... What you saw was a buck wearin' Lucy's dress. I found Lucy back in the canyon. Wrapped her in my coat, buried her with my own hands, I thought it best to keep it from ya.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Brad Jorgensen: Did they...? Was she...?
clean: Did they...? Was she...?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Ethan: What do you want me to do? Draw you a picture? Spell it out? Don't ever ask me! Long as you live, don't ever ask me more.
clean: What do you want me to do? Draw you a picture? Spell it out? Don't ever ask me! Long as you live, don't ever ask me more.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Reverend Clayton: Well, the prodigal brother. Ain't seen you since the surrender. Come to think of it, I didn't see you at the surrender.
clean: Well, the prodigal brother. Ain't seen you since the surrender. Come to think of it, I didn't see you at the surrender.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Ethan: I don't believe in surrenders.
clean: I don't believe in surrenders.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Brad: There's only one way you can stop me from looking for Lucy, mister, and that's kill me!
clean: There's only one way you can stop me from looking for Lucy, mister, and that's kill me!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Ethan: Alright, but I'm giving the orders, hear. I'm giving the orders or we're splitting up right here and now!
clean: Alright, but I'm giving the orders, hear. I'm giving the orders or we're splitting up right here and now!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Martin Pawley: Well sure Ethan. The only real reason were here ain't it - is to find Debbie and Lucy?
clean: Well sure Ethan. The only real reason were here ain't it - is to find Debbie and Lucy?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Ethan: If they're still alive.
clean: If they're still alive.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Reverend Clayton: Jorgenson!
clean: Jorgenson!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Ethan: Why don't you finish the job?
clean: Why don't you finish the job?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Reverend Clayton: What good did that do ya?
clean: What good did that do ya?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Ethan: By what you preach... none. But, what that Comanch believes - ain't got no eyes... can't enter the spirit land... has to wander forever between the winds. You get it Reverend.
clean: By what you preach... none. But, what that Comanch believes - ain't got no eyes... can't enter the spirit land... has to wander forever between the winds. You get it Reverend.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Martin: You know, Laurie, I was just thinking that maybe it's about time you and me started going steady, huh?
clean: You know, Laurie, I was just thinking that maybe it's about time you and me started going steady, huh?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Laurie Jorgensen: Why Martin Pawley, you and me been going steady since we was three years old!
clean: Why Martin Pawley, you and me been going steady since we was three years old!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Martin: We have?
clean: We have?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Laurie: 'Bout time you found out about it.
clean: 'Bout time you found out about it.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Laurie: [Martin is preparing to join a raid against the Indians and rescue Debbie] You're not goin', not this time.
clean: You're not goin', not this time.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Martin: Are you crazy?
clean: Are you crazy?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Laurie: It's too late. She's a woman grown now.
clean: It's too late. She's a woman grown now.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Martin: But I gotta go, Laurie, I gotta fetch her home.
clean: But I gotta go, Laurie, I gotta fetch her home.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Laurie: Fetch what home? The leavings a Comanche buck sold time and again to the highest bidder, with savage brats of her own?
clean: Fetch what home? The leavings a Comanche buck sold time and again to the highest bidder, with savage brats of her own?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Martin: [upset] Laurie, shut your mouth.
clean: Laurie, shut your mouth.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Laurie: Do you know what Ethan will do if he has a chance? He'll put a bullet in her brain.
clean: Do you know what Ethan will do if he has a chance? He'll put a bullet in her brain.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Laurie: I tell you, Martha would want him to.
clean: I tell you, Martha would want him to.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Martin: Only if I'm dead.
clean: Only if I'm dead.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Martin: [speaking to Debbie for the first time in five years] Debbie... Debbie, Debbie, don't you remember? I'm Martin, I'm Martin, your brother, remember? Debbie, remember back.
clean: Debbie... Debbie, Debbie, don't you remember? I'm Martin, I'm Martin, your brother, remember? Debbie, remember back.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Martin: Do you remember how I used to let you ride my horse? And tell you stories?
clean: Do you remember how I used to let you ride my horse? And tell you stories?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Martin: Oh don't you remember me, Debbie?
clean: Oh don't you remember me, Debbie?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Debbie Edwards: [sad] I remember, from always. At first I prayed to you to come and get me, take me home. You didn't come.
clean: I remember, from always. At first I prayed to you to come and get me, take me home. You didn't come.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Martin: But I've come now, Debbie.
clean: But I've come now, Debbie.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Debbie: These are my people... Go. Go, Martin, please!
clean: These are my people... Go. Go, Martin, please!
